note: Phase 7 — honest reconciliation of the prior canary-closure live-call ledgers. Zero paid calls this sprint. The append-BEFORE-dispatch pattern reserved a slot before each provider call, so a reserved-but-failed dispatch is indistinguishable from a billed call using the ledger alone.

corrected_semantics.rule: Check the cap BEFORE reserving. A blocked-cap attempt is NOT a provider call. callsConsumed counts ONLY confirmed provider dispatches; refused runs go to a separate blockedAttempts list. Each paid-call record carries seq, role, requested/served model, status, reservedAt, completedAt, HTTP/strict outcome, prompt/completion tokens, latency.
corrected_semantics.applies_to: future live harnesses (canary-runtime-closure-v2 already records reservedAt via append-before-dispatch; the reservation-vs-dispatch distinction is documented here and enforced going forward).

reconciliation.evidence: run 1 architect; a critic dispatch (seq 2) followed in the same run, which only happens after a completed architect response
run 1 critic; the run produced a grounded shadow result

a second architect reservation ~2 minutes later (pre-fix harness iteration). No critic followed, so it is NOT provable from the ledger whether the provider actually dispatched+billed this reservation or it was reserved-then-aborted. Provider billing/logs were not captured. Marked UNKNOWN — not paid, not unpaid.

prior_ledgers.note: The successful outcome-A closure — a clean 2-dispatch run (architect + critic), both completed.
